title: Tuple Unpacking
description: Learn tuple unpacking — one of Python's most elegant features. Write functions that swap two values without a temporary variable, split a tuple into its first element and the rest, unpack a list of coordinate tuples into separate x and y lists, and perform multiple assignment in a single line.
starterCode:
def swap_values(a, b):
    """Return a tuple (b, a) — the two values swapped."""
    pass


def split_first(t):
    """Return a tuple (first, rest) where first is t[0]
    and rest is a tuple of the remaining elements."""
    pass


def unpack_coordinates(points_list):
    """Given a list of (x, y) tuples, return two tuples:
    one with all x values and one with all y values.
    E.g., [(1,2),(3,4)] -> ((1,3), (2,4))"""
    pass


def multi_assign():
    """Use tuple unpacking to assign name='Python',
    version=3, year=1991 in a single line.
    Return them as a tuple (name, version, year)."""
    pass
hints: Python allows swapping with a, b = b, a — no temporary variable needed.
Use slicing t[1:] to get all elements after the first one.
You can unpack tuples inside a for loop: for x, y in points_list.
Multiple assignment works by placing comma-separated names on the left side of = and comma-separated values on the right.

testCases.description: unpack_coordinates separates x and y values
split_first with two-element tuple returns single-element rest
